justification: Generally accurate
Missing detailed migration steps and leaves out key audit-before-cache security measures.
reference case validation was not covered.
Mostly acceptable, though Fix Report Accuracy and Security Coverage could be stronger
Core zero-trust components are well laid out, though specific jitter and relay details are absent.
The data_model section and fix_report were entirely omitted. The immutable PCI audit log (ISS-002) and dual-write specifics were not addressed.
The critical feature_column_map requirement was overlooked and no fix_report was included
Lays a solid TUI foundation but omits the fix_report and the required local metrics binding.
Core GCP components are correctly identified, though the async Cloud Tasks and min_instances optimizations from the reference are absent.
GDPR consent requirement (ISS-001) was not addressed and the fix_report was missing.
High-level OTLP components are well structured and provide a sound base for Rust-based gRPC ingestion.
Java library components are well organized, but the critical SEQUENTIAL_ONLY lock enforcement is missing.
The bare-metal GPU research direction is maintained, however critical HIPAA compliance and LLM-leakage remediation were not included
The fix_report was not included and the critical append-only audit log trigger was not implemented.
The core Go/AKS migration structure is well established with a strong foundation for Azure AD integration.
Core AWS serverless ETL components are correctly identified with a coherent flow for CRO data ingestion.
Solid static framework provided, but the critical Pagefind lazy-loading integration is missing
Core Azure migration services are correctly identified with a logical flow for ERP data transition.
Core real-time components are present but the async ETA engine decoupling and database partitioning specifics are absent.
High-level MWAA/Snowflake framework is well established with a reliable baseline for county-specific normalization.
Core Next.js/Vercel framework is well established with a clear baseline for developer documentation.
Core Python/Azure framework is solidly defined with a good baseline for AD enumeration and remediation.
Provides a strong foundational framework for the ML pipeline with a clear baseline for crisis classification.
Core GCP components are well identified, though the specific rate-limiting and cross-marketplace reconciliation details from the reference are absent.

Good alignment on the Tauri 2/Rust framework and IPC strategies. Core optimization and encryption requirements are correctly identified, though the regulatory reporting structure is slightly less detailed.
High-level Go/EKS framework is well established with a strong baseline for CME feed ingestion.
Good alignment on the Astro 4/Netlify stack. The zero-JS-by-default philosophy and Edge Functions usage for lightweight interactivity are correctly identified.
Good alignment on the Next.js/Contentful/Vercel stack. The zero-runtime static-export approach and webhook-driven rebuild pattern are correctly identified.
Good alignment on the Astro 4/Vercel stack. The zero-JS-by-default approach and Edge Functions for lead processing are correctly captured.

The Tauri 2/Rust/React stack and Konva.js map engine are correctly captured. However, the specific state-loss issue (ISS-001) and schema migration strategy (ISS-002) were not addressed, resulting in a generic desktop architecture.

Good alignment on the Astro/Contentful/Cloudflare stack. The independent serverless function for lead relay and the webhook-driven rebuild pattern are correctly identified, though the specific P95 rebuild SLA is absent.

Good alignment on the FastAPI/Celery/Kubernetes stack. Worker pool specialization and hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) constraints are correctly identified, though the specific 'SKIP LOCKED' concurrency strategy is missing.

The critical SecurityEventHandler ABC finalization (ISS-001) is not addressed and the session-id validation strategy is incorrectly attributed to SHA-256. A generic library structure is provided rather than the specialized security-hardened design required.

Core real-time telemetry and WPF/SignalR stack are captured accurately. Hardware integration points (TCP/Serial) are correctly identified, though the specific GPX-to-km projection algorithm critical for race profile visualization is absent.
Good alignment on the EMR/Snowflake/OMOP stack. The medallion architecture and Great Expectations data quality gates are correctly identified.

The cffi/WASM integration is successfully captured, but the critical concurrency issues (GIL release and instance pooling) from the reference are not addressed, resulting in a non-concurrent single-threaded design.

The offline-first React Native/WatermelonDB stack and Go/ECS backend are correctly captured. However, the two-phase sync requirement (ISS-001) for photos and the conflict review UX (ISS-002) are not addressed, with generic sync and versioning logic provided instead.

Core Python library components and the need for high-performance lookups are correctly identified. However, the 'halt-on-missing' error hierarchy (ISS-001) and the pytest-benchmark CI gate configuration (ISS-002) required by the reference are absent.

Spring Boot/Spring Batch core and multi-tier AWS architecture are correctly identified. However, the specialized compliance logic (pre-computed threshold flags) and high-performance pagination strategy required for large-scale auditor queries are absent.

The Flutter/TFLite/FastAPI stack is correctly captured, including offline SQLite buffering and Firebase custom claims for role-based access. However, GDPR consent versioning (ISS-002) and TFLite pre-warming (ISS-001) technical requirements are not addressed.

The Kafka/EKS stack and multi-tiered state management (Redis + Cassandra) are correctly captured. However, the critical 'Data Minimization' strategy (GPS TTL) and the per-vehicle Cognito authentication flow required by the reference are absent.

Good alignment on the Docusaurus/S3/CloudFront stack. The zero-runtime static philosophy and Algolia search integration are correctly identified, though the specific CloudFront invalidation and CSP fix details are missing.

Critical implementation details for rate limiting (Token Bucket) and inactivity thresholds (ISS-002) are absent. The output is a generic CLI structure lacking the domain-specific safety guards (YES confirmation, countdown) defined in the reference.

The plan is notably sparse and fails to cover the critical technical requirement (ISS-001) for extracting OEM curves into a dbt seed. The data model is also entirely omitted, leaving the relational structure for the pipeline unestablished.

The Next.js/Hono/ECS stack and the key performance strategy (RSC) are correctly identified. However, the 'Read Replica' isolation for 2-year history and the NACHA-compliant step-up authentication flow required for HNW financial portals are missing.

The plan aligns well with the Rust/pest/Clap stack, correctly identifying the simulation logic and the provenance sidecar approach over a database. GLP-specific requirements and physical instrument dispatch logic from the reference are slightly less detailed.

The AKS/Azure OpenAI/pgvector stack and core RAG workflow are correctly captured. However, the specific IVFFlat indexing strategy and the detailed audit log structure (prompt hashing) required for production performance and compliance are absent.

The predicted plan successfully identifies the core Go/SSH stack and the local storage strategy. However, it fails to address the critical CGO/Static-Binary conflict (SQLCipher) and lacks the specific NIST 800-53 control mappings required for the compliance context.
